info.name: Research Workflow Service
info.description:
Multi-agent research workflow orchestrator — Dorsia v2.

Workflow lifecycle:
1. POST /workflows — kicks off research (Gemini) → review (Claude) → resolve → consensus loop
2. WS  /ws/workflows/{workflowId} — stream real-time events + user chat
3. POST /approve or /request-changes — move to final report generation

State machine: INITIATED → RESEARCHING → RESEARCH_COMPLETE → REVIEWING → REVIEW_COMPLETE → RESOLVING → RESOLUTION_COMPLETE → RE_REVIEWING → CONSENSUS_REACHED → USER_REVIEW → USER_APPROVED → GENERATING_FINAL → COMPLETED

request.description: Service info — returns name, version, docs URL.
Returns service health: gateway connectivity + database status.

Start a new research workflow. The orchestrator begins the INITIATED → RESEARCHING pipeline immediately.

**Fields:**
- `topic` (required) — The research question or topic.
- `context` — Optional background to guide the researcher agent.
- `depth` — `shallow` | `standard` | `deep` (controls Gemini research thoroughness).
- `max_review_cycles` — Caps review/resolve iterations (default 5).
- `output_format` — `markdown` (default).
- `agent_config` — Per-agent overrides (model, persona, etc.).

query.description: Filter by state (e.g. RESEARCHING, USER_REVIEW, COMPLETED)

request.description: List all workflows with optional state filter and pagination.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "workflows": [
    {
      "workflow_id": "wf-0",
      "topic": "Impact of LLMs on SE productivity",
      "context": "",
      "depth": "deep",
      "current_state": "RESEARCHING",
      "previous_state": "INITIATED",
      "review_cycle": 0,
      "forced_consensus": false,
      "workspace_path": "./workspace/wf-0",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21T10:00:00+00:00",
      "updated_at": "2026-03-21T10:00:05+00:00",
      "completed_at": null
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 0
}

request.description: Filter workflows by a specific state (e.g. USER_REVIEW to find those awaiting user action).
Get detailed info for a single workflow.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:

request.description: Detailed state: includes full state transition history and list of report artifacts.
Cancel a running workflow. Stops all agent tasks and transitions to CANCELLED state.
Get the latest draft report content (markdown). Available after RESEARCH_COMPLETE.
Get the final report file path and download URL. Only available after COMPLETED state.
Get all review cycles with structured comments. Each cycle includes the reviewer's consensus flag, quality score, summary, and per-section comments.

response.name: 200 OK

query.description: Filter by agent role (researcher, reviewer, gemini_resolver, claude_resolver, user_chat, report_generator)

request.description: Get the full conversation log for a workflow. Optionally filter by agent role.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Get only conversations from a specific agent role.

item.name: User Actions
item.description: Actions the user takes during USER_REVIEW state to move the workflow forward.

request.description:
User approves the report. Transitions from USER_REVIEW → USER_APPROVED → GENERATING_FINAL → COMPLETED.

Only valid when workflow is in USER_REVIEW state.

response.name: 400 Invalid State

request.description:
User requests changes. Sends the workflow back through the review/resolve cycle (up to max_user_change_requests).

The `changes` object is free-form — the resolver agents interpret it as instructions.

item.name: WebSocket — Real-time Events
item.description:
WebSocket connection for streaming workflow events and user chat.

**Connect:** `ws://localhost:8000/ws/workflows/{workflowId}`

Use Postman's WebSocket Request tab to test these.

**Server → Client event types:**
- `connection.state` — Initial state on connect
- `workflow.state_changed` — State transition
- `agent.stream_delta` — Streaming text chunk from an agent
- `agent.status` — Agent lifecycle (started, completed, failed)
- `review.comments` — Structured review output
- `resolution.merged` — Merged resolver output
- `workflow.completed` — Final completion
- `workflow.error` — Error event
- `user.chat_response` — Streaming chat response
- `user.approval_accepted` — Approval confirmed
- `user.changes_accepted` — Changes accepted
- `error` — Error message

**Client → Server message types:**
- `user.message` — Send chat message during USER_REVIEW
- `user.approve` — Approve the report
- `user.request_changes` — Request changes
- `user.cancel` — Cancel the workflow

request.description:
**This is a WebSocket endpoint — use Postman's WebSocket Request tab, not this HTTP request.**

URL: `ws://localhost:8000/ws/workflows/{{workflowId}}`

On connect, the server sends the current workflow state as the first message.

item.name: WS › Send Chat Message (copy as raw body)
WS › Approve Report (copy as raw body)
WS › Request Changes (copy as raw body)
WS › Cancel Workflow (copy as raw body)
E2E Flow — Happy Path
item.description: Run these requests in order to test the full workflow lifecycle.

request.description: Poll this endpoint to track state progression. In production, use the WebSocket instead.
